hero.badge: A leading CPA firm in Israel
hero.subtitle: Asor — Certified Public Accountants. Professionalism, transparency, and personal guidance that handles your business at every scale — from the wide view to the smallest detail.

activityAreas.title: End to end — at every scale
activityAreas.subtitle: Six core practice areas that keep your business healthy, organized, and ready for any decision.

audit.title: Audit & Financial Reporting
audit.description: Independent, professional audits for companies and nonprofits — aligned with the strictest local and international accounting standards.

bookkeeping.title: Bookkeeping & Payroll
bookkeeping.description: Ongoing, professional management of your books and payroll, with tight controls and transparent reporting at every step.

tax.title: Taxation & Tax Planning
tax.description: Smart tax planning, representation before tax authorities, and tax refund handling for both self-employed and salaried clients.

consulting.title: Business Consulting
consulting.description: Strategic guidance through every stage of business — from launch through growth, exit, and intergenerational transfer.

realestate.title: Real Estate & Urban Renewal
realestate.description: A unique specialty in evacuation-and-rebuild (Pinui-Binui), TAMA 38, and the taxation of real estate transactions.

nonprofits.title: Nonprofits & NGOs
nonprofits.description: Comprehensive support for nonprofits and public-benefit corporations, including proper-management certification and routine reporting.

about.title: About the firm
about.subtitle: Asor — Certified Public Accountants, founded on the belief that quality financial guidance is much more than tallying numbers at year-end.
about.p1: Our firm provides personal, professional, and comprehensive service, tailored precisely to each client's financial needs. We stand for reliability, transparency, and the pursuit of excellence — and we accompany our clients at every stage of business life.
about.p2: Our team combines deep professional knowledge with a personal, attentive approach. We believe that every client deserves direct access to a professional who knows their business deeply, and who can support strategic decisions — not just technical ones.
about.p3: Alongside our core CPA services — audit, bookkeeping, and taxation — we have developed a unique specialty in real estate, Pinui-Binui, and TAMA 38, an area that requires combined knowledge of accounting, taxation, and the complexities of the Israeli real estate world.

privacy.title: Privacy & cookies policy
privacy.subtitle: We are committed to protecting your privacy in accordance with the Israeli Privacy Protection Law, 1981 and its regulations.
privacy.intro: This privacy policy (the "Policy") describes how Asor — Certified Public Accountants (the "Firm", "we") collects, holds, uses, and transfers personal information received via this site, and your rights regarding such information. Using the site and submitting information through it constitutes consent to the terms of this Policy, subject to any applicable law.
privacy.controllerInfo: Database owner and holder: Asor — Certified Public Accountants, Company No. 515367779. Address: 17 Nahum Heftzadi St., Jerusalem, Ram Tower, Floor 11. Privacy Coordinator: Orit Asor — for any matter related to this Policy or to your statutory rights, by phone 052-3975659 or email office@za-cpa.com.
privacy.lawText: Collection and processing of personal information on this site is conducted in accordance with the Israeli Privacy Protection Law, 1981 (the "Law"), the Privacy Protection (Information Security) Regulations, 2017, the Privacy Protection Law (Amendment No. 13), 2024, and where relevant — the Israeli Communications Law (Telecommunications and Broadcasting), 1982 with respect to direct marketing.
privacy.collectIntro: We collect the following types of information, only to the extent necessary:
collectItems: Information you provide voluntarily through site forms: full name, email, phone, and message content
Technical information collected automatically when you visit the site: IP address, browser type and version, operating system, screen resolution, referrer, and pages viewed
Personal preferences saved in cookies in your browser: interface language, accessibility settings, and cookie category consents
If you allow analytics cookies — aggregate, non-identifiable statistical information about usage patterns
privacy.collectNote: Information is collected directly from you or automatically through your browser. We do not collect sensitive information (as defined in Section 7 of the Law — such as health, political views, or religious beliefs) through this site.
privacy.useTitle: Purposes for which information is collected
useItems: Responding to inquiries, professional engagement, and handling service requests
Managing the ongoing relationship with clients and prospective clients
Improving the site and the firm's services and analyzing aggregate usage patterns
Compliance with legal, regulatory, and professional obligations applicable to CPAs, including bookkeeping and document retention
Information security, fraud detection, and prevention of misuse
If you have given express consent — sending marketing materials, professional newsletters, or updates
privacy.legalBasisIntro: We process personal information on one or more of the following bases:
legalBasisItems: Your express consent — when you fill out a contact form, accept optional cookies, or join a mailing list
Performance of an engagement with you — for the provision of the professional services you requested
Legal obligation — including reporting, bookkeeping, and document retention duties imposed on the firm by tax law, the CPA Law, 1955, and other laws
The firm's legitimate interest — service improvement, information security, and prevention of misuse — balanced against your rights
privacy.cookiesIntro: Cookies are small text files saved in your browser when you visit the site. We use cookies in three categories, as detailed below. Essential cookies do not require consent. Optional cookies require your explicit consent, managed via the cookie banner.
privacy.cookiesEssential: Essential (Strictly Necessary) cookies: required for proper site operation and cannot be disabled. They include language choice, accessibility settings, cookie consents, and session identification.
privacy.cookiesAnalytics: Analytics cookies: allow us to understand how visitors use the site based on aggregated, non-identifiable data. Loaded only after your consent.
privacy.cookiesMarketing: Marketing cookies: may be used to tailor content and measure advertising effectiveness, including through external partners. Loaded only after your express consent.
privacy.cookiesRetention: Cookie retention varies between session-only and longer durations (up to 12 months). You can delete cookies at any time via your browser settings.
privacy.cookiesManage: You can update or withdraw your consent for optional cookies at any time using the "Manage consent" button at the bottom of every page. Withdrawal of consent does not affect the lawfulness of processing carried out before withdrawal.
privacy.retentionText: We retain personal information for the period required for the purposes for which it was collected, or longer where required by law. In particular: documents related to professional services are retained as required by tax law and the CPA Law (typically not less than 7 years from the end of the tax year). Inquiries that did not lead to engagement are kept for up to 24 months for documentation and follow-up. Upon expiration of the retention period, the information is deleted or anonymized.
privacy.thirdPartyText: We do not sell, rent, or trade your personal information. Information may be transferred to third parties only in the following circumstances: (1) infrastructure and service providers acting on our behalf (site hosting, information security, email and analytics providers), subject to their contractual confidentiality obligations and compliance with the Information Security Regulations; (2) competent authorities — where required by law or judicial order; (3) in the course of professional service delivery — to tax authorities, the Companies Registrar, or other parties with your consent.
privacy.internationalText: The site is hosted on GitHub Pages, and as a result certain technical information (including IP addresses) may be processed on servers located outside Israel. Such transfer is conducted in accordance with the Privacy Protection Regulations (Transfer of Information to Databases Outside the State Borders), 2001, to countries with adequate privacy-protection arrangements or based on equivalent contractual safeguards.
privacy.directMarketingText: If you choose to receive newsletters, professional materials, or marketing messages from us, we will send them only after receiving your express consent and in accordance with Section 30A of the Israeli Communications Law (Telecommunications and Broadcasting), 1982 and Section 17F of the Law. Every such message will include a simple option to unsubscribe, and we commit to honor unsubscribe requests without delay.
privacy.minorsText: The site is not intended to collect information from minors under the age of 16, and we do not knowingly collect personal information from them without parental or legal-guardian consent. If you become aware that a minor has submitted information through the site without appropriate consent, please contact us and we will act promptly to delete the information.
privacy.rightsIntro: The Law grants you the following rights regarding personal information held about you:
rightsItems: Right of access — Section 13 of the Law: the right to receive information about what is held about you
Right of correction or deletion — Section 14 of the Law: request to correct inaccurate, outdated, or incomplete information, or to delete it where appropriate
Right to withdraw consent — including for optional cookies or marketing, at any time
Right to opt out of direct marketing — Section 17F of the Law and Section 30A of the Communications Law
Right to file a complaint with the Privacy Protection Authority — if you believe your rights have been infringed
privacy.rightsContact: To exercise your rights, contact us using the details at the bottom of this page. We will address your request within 30 days; if additional time is required we will inform you accordingly.
privacy.securityText: We take reasonable technical and organizational security measures in accordance with the Privacy Protection (Information Security) Regulations, 2017, including encrypted transport (HTTPS), access control, security monitoring, and ongoing software updates. In the event of a serious security incident (as defined in the Regulations), we will notify the Privacy Protection Authority and affected individuals as required. However, no online method of safeguarding information guarantees absolute security.
privacy.complaintTitle: Right to file a complaint with the Privacy Protection Authority
privacy.complaintText: If you believe your right to privacy has been infringed and you have not received an appropriate response from us, you may file a complaint with the Privacy Protection Authority at the Israeli Ministry of Justice, via the Authority's website (privacy.gov.il) or by mail: Privacy Protection Authority, P.O.B. 27338, Jerusalem 9127202.
privacy.changesText: We may update this Policy from time to time, including due to changes in legislation, technology, or our services. The current version is the one published on the site, alongside the last-updated date. In the event of a material change to the Policy, we will take reasonable steps to notify active users.
privacy.contactIntro: For any privacy-related question, request, or comment — including the exercise of statutory rights — please contact our Privacy Coordinator:
privacy.updatedText: This Policy was last updated on {{date}}. The most current version is always available on this page.
privacy.disclaimerText: This document is intended to describe our privacy practices in plain language. In the event of any inconsistency between this document and applicable law, the law shall prevail. Nothing in this Policy diminishes any of your rights under any applicable law.

statement.title: Accessibility statement
statement.subtitle: We are committed to making this site accessible to all users, including people with disabilities.
statement.intro: Asor — Certified Public Accountants places great importance on providing equitable and accessible service to all clients, including people with disabilities. The site is being made accessible as part of our effort to enable every individual to receive quality service independently and equally, in accordance with the Israeli Equal Rights for Persons with Disabilities Law (1998) and the Equal Rights for Persons with Disabilities Regulations (Accessibility Adjustments to a Service), 2013.
statement.standardText: The site is built to meet Israeli Standard 5568 at level AA, which is based on the W3C WCAG 2.0 guidelines. Accessibility adaptations are reviewed and improved on an ongoing basis.
features: A floating accessibility menu available on every page
Semantic heading structure and keyboard navigation
Sufficient color contrast between text and background
statement.limitationsTitle: Areas that are not yet accessible
statement.limitations: Some sections of the site may not yet be fully accessible. We are working to complete accessibility as soon as possible.
statement.coordinatorIntro: Found an accessibility issue? We'd appreciate hearing from you so we can address it promptly. You can contact our accessibility coordinator:
statement.responseText: We commit to responding to accessibility inquiries within 14 business days.
statement.updatedText: This statement was last updated on {{date}}.
